prompt: Add GitHub PR status widget showing open PRs for a repository

turns.content:
<task>
Add a GitHub PR status widget that shows open pull requests for a configured repository.
</task>

1	 import requests
2	 from typing import List, Dict
3	 
4	 class BuildStatusWidget:
5	     def __init__(self, ci_url: str, token: str):
6	         self.ci_url = ci_url
7	         self.session = requests.Session()
8	         self.session.headers['Authorization'] = f'Bearer {token}'
9	 
10	     def get_builds(self, project: str) -> List[Dict]:
11	         resp = self.session.get(f'{self.ci_url}/api/builds', params={'project': project})
12	         resp.raise_for_status()
13	         return resp.json()['builds']

arguments.query: GitHub REST API list pull requests endpoint authentication token

turns.content:
1. GitHub REST API - Pull Requests - https://docs.github.com/en/rest/pulls/pulls
   List pull requests: GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls. Supports state, sort, direction params.
2. GitHub API Authentication - https://docs.github.com/en/rest/authentication
   Use Authorization: Bearer <token> header with personal access token.
3. GitHub API Rate Limiting - https://docs.github.com/en/rest/rate-limit
   Authenticated: 5000 requests/hour. Unauthenticated: 60/hour.

arguments.title: GitHub REST API - Pull Requests
arguments.content:
GET https://api.github.com/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls
Params: state (open/closed/all), sort (created/updated/popularity), direction (asc/desc), per_page (max 100)
Auth: Authorization: Bearer <token>
Response: array of PR objects with title, number, user.login, html_url, created_at, head.ref, base.ref
Rate limit: 5000/hour authenticated

turns.content: Finding recorded: GitHub REST API - Pull Requests

arguments.summary: Researched GitHub PR API. Will create widget following BuildStatusWidget pattern with requests.Session.

arguments.command: cd /project && python -c "from widgets.github_prs import GitHubPRWidget; print('Import OK')"

arguments.message: GitHub PR widget is ready. It fetches the 10 most recently updated open PRs. You'll need to pass a GitHub personal access token and repo string (e.g., 'owner/repo'). The widget follows the same requests.Session pattern as BuildStatusWidget.

arguments.summary: Created GitHubPRWidget in src/widgets/github_prs.py. Fetches open PRs via GitHub REST API with Bearer token auth. Returns number, title, author, URL, and branch for each PR.
arguments.final_answer: GitHub PR widget created. Fetches open PRs sorted by last updated. Uses Bearer token authentication. Returns PR number, title, author, URL, and branch name.
